SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE

Agency: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Savannah District
Synopsis Number: W912HN-25-SS-0043
Title: Facility Operations and Maintenance Support Services – Fort Stewart and Hunter Army Airfield, Georgia
NAICS Code: 561210 – Facilities Support Services
Size Standard: $47.0M
Response Deadline: February 14, 2025, 4:00 PM ET

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Savannah District, is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns capable of providing comprehensive facility operations and maintenance services at Fort Stewart and Hunter Army Airfield, Georgia. Services include HVAC maintenance, plumbing, electrical systems repair, grounds maintenance, pest control, and refuse collection across approximately 1,200 facilities.

Based on market research results, the Government anticipates issuing this requirement as a HUBZone set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305 if two or more responsible HUBZone small business concerns are identified that can perform the work at fair market prices. The HUBZone price evaluation preference in accordance with FAR 19.1307 may also apply.

Interested firms must submit capability statements addressing the following:

(a) Company name, CAGE code, DUNS/UEI number, and current SBA HUBZone certification status (include certification date and SBA case number);
(b) Relevant past performance on contracts of similar size, scope, and complexity performed within the last five (5) years, including contract numbers, dollar values, and points of contact;
(c) Description of technical capability and available resources;
(d) Confirmation of ability to meet bonding requirements up to $5,000,000.

This notice is for market research and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation or commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or award a contract. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this notice.

A determination that only one SDVOSB can meet the requirement is not required. This notice is not a request for competitive quotes.

C.4 VAAR 852.219-73 VA NOTICE OF TOTAL SET-ASIDE FOR CERTIFIED SERVICE-DISABLED VETERAN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESSES (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION)

(a) Definition. for the Department of Veterans Affairs, ''Service-disabled Veteran-owned small business concern or SDVOSB'':
(1) Means a small business concern—
(i) Not less than 51 percent of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or, in the case of any publicly owned business, not less than 51 percent of the stock of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or eligible surviving spouses (see VAAR 802.201, Surviving Spouse definition);
(ii) The management and daily business operations of which are controlled by one or more service-disabled Veterans (or eligible surviving spouses) or, in the case of a service-disabled Veteran with permanent and severe disability, the spouse or permanent caregiver of such Veteran;
(iii) The business meets Federal small business size standards for the applicable North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code identified in the solicitation document;
(iv) The business has been certified for ownership and control pursuant to 38 U.S.C. 8127, 13 CFR 128, and is listed as certified in the SBA certification database at https://veterans.certify.sba.gov/; and
(v) The business agrees to comply with VAAR subpart 819.70 and Small Business Administration (SBA) regulations regarding small business size, government contracting, and the Veteran Small Business Certification Program at 13 CFR parts 121, 125, and 128.

(b) General. In order for a concern to submit an offer and be eligible for the award of an SDVOSB set-aside or sole source contract, the concern must qualify as a small business concern under the size standard corresponding to the NAICS code assigned to the contract and be listed as an SDVOSB participant in the SBA certification database as set forth in 13 CFR 128.
(1) Offers received from entities that are not certified SDVOSBs and listed in the SBA certification database at the time of offer shall not be considered.
(2) Any award resulting from this solicitation shall be made to a certified SDVOSB listed in the SBA certification database who is eligible at the time of submission of offer(s) and at the time of award.

This requirement is set aside exclusively for participants in the Small Business Administration (SBA) 8(a) Business Development Program as an 8(a) competitive set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.805-2 and the SBA/GSA Partnership Agreement. This solicitation incorporates the following provisions and clauses by reference: FAR 52.219-11, Special 8(a) Contract Conditions (DEC 2023); FAR 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting (OCT 2023); FAR 52.219-18, Notification of Competition Limited to Eligible 8(a) Participants (OCT 2023).

Size Standard: $47,000,000
Contract Type: Firm-Fixed-Price with Economic Price Adjustment
Estimated Total Contract Value: $14,500,000
Period of Performance: Base Year + Four (4) Option Years

The contractor shall provide comprehensive building operations, preventive and corrective maintenance, janitorial services, grounds maintenance, and energy management for the 900,000 gross square foot federal complex. Services include HVAC system operations, electrical distribution maintenance, fire and life safety systems testing, elevator maintenance oversight, and 24/7 building automation system monitoring. The contractor shall provide a qualified on-site Building Manager and sufficient trade staff to maintain the facility at GSA Outcome-Based standards.

7. EVALUATION AND BASIS FOR AWARD (LPTA)
Award will be made in accordance with FAR 13.106-2 using a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process. The Government intends to establish a single award with a responsible ISBEE vendor whose quotation is determined to be technically acceptable and offers the lowest evaluated price.

Under the LPTA process, quotations will not be ranked or scored. Instead, quotations will be evaluated on an acceptable/unacceptable basis for non-price factors. Award will be made to the vendor whose quotation:
• Is determined to be technically acceptable under all non-price factors,
• Is compliant with all solicitation requirements,
• Is determined responsible in accordance with FAR Part 9, and
• Offers the lowest evaluated price.

7.1 Eligibility and Compliance Review
Quotations will first be evaluated for eligibility and compliance with all solicitation requirements. This review will include:
• Verification of ISBEE eligibility status
• Submission of the required IEE Representative Form
• Completion of all required representations and certifications
• Adherence to quotation preparation instructions
Quotations that fail to meet solicitation requirements or are determined noncompliant will be eliminated from further consideration.

A NOTE FROM THE CTO

To deliver on its current and future mission, NGA needs to deliver useful software faster and more consistently. To do this, the NGA Technology Strategy describes changes in approach, tools, and behavior that will result in faster iterations, more flexible products, and better-satisfied users and customers. The NGA Software Way is an implementation guide to supplement NGA's Technology Strategy and provide additional specific guidance for teams building and operating software.

Our intent is to get to a place where NGA software products are designed and built with users, can be automatically updated in production daily, and use product-specific metrics to make decisions. We believe in small, empowered, cross-functional teams that deeply understand their users and constantly work directly with those users to deliver on their mission.

Implementing The NGA Software Way won't happen all at once; we must start with iteratively tracking and improving these metrics and meeting each element below. As we do this together, and with an understanding of this intent, we will successfully deliver software products with the speed, accuracy, and precision NGA's mission demands.

The three initial metrics all programs must track for each software product delivered to users are availability, lead time for changes, and deployment frequency. It is important to note that NGA is not unique in using these metrics. These are three of the primary metrics DevOps Research and Assessment (DORA) has studied over the past six years. These metrics provide visibility into how NGA balances speed (deployment frequency and lead time for changes) and stability (availability) during product development and operations.

4.1 General Guidelines
In Phase 2, companies shall provide an in-person pitch at their proposed location or other location agreed upon by the company and the Government to demonstrate and provide further details on their proposed concept / technology / solution and the technical and business viability of the proposed solution. The Government may also leverage external market research during Phase 2 to evaluate a company's viability.

In addition to the Pitch, the Government, at its discretion, may request for consideration an additional written submission to further supplement or clarify the information provided in the Phase 1 Solution Briefs. The Government, at its discretion, may elect to release additional information to companies that have been selected for the pitch.

The pitch format will be as follows:
(1) Up to a 90-minute presentation provided by the company, the Government may request an audio recording of this presentation
(2) Up to a 90-minute question and answer between the Government and company
(3) Up to a 120-minute site visit / tour of facilities by company, if the company location is selected

Individual pitches will be evaluated based on the merit of the proposed concept in addressing the SOW.

• Organizational breakdown

The Contractor shall perform boundary and topographic surveys covering approximately 340 acres across three separate tracts adjacent to Eufaula Lake project lands. Deliverables shall include monumentation of boundary corners with capped rebar, ALTA/NSPS-compliant survey plats in both PDF and AutoCAD Civil 3D formats, legal descriptions, and a written report summarizing findings, discrepancies, and any encroachments identified. All surveys shall reference the Oklahoma State Plane Coordinate System, South Zone, NAD 83 (2011 adjustment), NAVD 88. The Contractor shall coordinate access through the Lake Manager's Office a minimum of two weeks prior to field activities.

6.10 - Testing and Quality Assurance (QA)

Testing and Quality Assurance (QA) activities ensure that modernized systems, applications, and infrastructure meet functional, performance, and security requirements, supporting mission-critical operations. These activities are embedded throughout the modernization lifecycle, from the initial refactoring of legacy code to the final stages of User Acceptance Testing (UAT). Comprehensive testing strategies validate that all components work seamlessly together, mitigate risks, and enhance system reliability. Leveraging frameworks like Test-Driven Development (TDD), Behavior-Driven Development (BDD), and automation tools such as Selenium, QA ensures thorough validation with increased efficiency. Additionally, generative AI accelerates the process by automating test case generation and identifying edge cases, reducing manual effort.

Testing and Quality Assurance support includes developing tailored testing strategies aligned with modernization goals, implementing automated frameworks for continuous testing, and providing performance and security validation to ensure systems meet stringent compliance standards. This approach integrates with DevOps practices through Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD), enabling rapid feedback cycles and minimizing risks during deployments.

Deliverables: Vendor to propose Testing and Quality Assurance (QA) deliverables including but not limited to: Comprehensive Testing Strategy Document, Generative AI-Driven Test Case Library, Automated Test Suites, Performance Testing Reports, UAT Scripts and Results, Security and Compliance Reports, and Real-Time Test Monitoring Dashboards.
